title: potentilla recta subsp. hirta
intro: Potentilla recta subsp. hirta, also known as the rough-leaved cinquefoil, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potent anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as wounds, burns, and skin infections. The plant's roots and leaves contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that offer health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can aid in the prevention and treatment of conditions like cardiovascular disease, cancer, and respiratory infections. Potentilla recta subsp. hirta can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
